description_long: This is a large, military-style helicopter with a streamlined, angular fuselage painted in a matte gray color. It features a prominent main rotor with four long, straight blades and a tail rotor mounted on the vertical stabilizer at the rear. The cockpit is located at the front, with multiple angular windows providing visibility for the pilots, and the body includes several access panels and hatches along the sides. The landing gear consists of robust, fixed wheels rather than skids, designed for stability on various surfaces. The tail section is elongated, with a horizontal stabilizer and a vertical fin supporting the tail rotor. The overall design suggests durability and functionality, suitable for urban or tactical operations.
description: A large, gray military-style helicopter with a four-blade main rotor, angular cockpit windows, and fixed wheel landing gear.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the helicopter, main and tail rotors, cockpit windows, and fixed wheel landing gear.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the angular cockpit windows, nose, and the symmetrical spread of the main rotor blades.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the tail section, rotor configuration, and side access panels.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the tail boom, tail rotor, and the alignment of the main rotor blades.
attribute_rotor_blades: Four long, straight main rotor blades, likely made of composite material, colored dark gray or black with no visible markings. The blades are evenly spaced and extend well beyond the width of the fuselage.
attribute_tail_rotor: Present on the vertical stabilizer at the rear, with four short blades arranged in a cross pattern, colored dark gray or black, with no visible protective shroud.
attribute_landing_skids: This helicopter uses fixed wheel landing gear instead of skids. The wheels are robust, black, and attached to sturdy struts beneath the fuselage.
attribute_cockpit_windows: Multiple angular cockpit windows at the front, including two large forward-facing panes and several smaller side windows, all framed in black and slightly tinted.
